---
name: rag-retrieval-tuner
description: 'Diagnose and improve retrieval quality for the local pgvector + Ollama + LangGraph stack using the evals harness as ground truth. Use when answers are off-topic or shallow, when adding/tuning a retrieval stage (rewrite, multi-query, HyDE, rerank), when chunk size or hybrid weights change, or when refreshing the router policy. Pairs with the rag-retrieval-tuner agent.'
---

# RAG Retrieval Tuning

Companion procedure for the [rag-retrieval-tuner](../../agents/rag-retrieval-tuner.md) agent.
This codebase has a real evals harness ([evals/run_evals.py](../../../evals/run_evals.py))
that writes scored result files and a `policy.json` consumed by the router —
treat it as the source of truth and **never tune by vibes**.

## When to Use

- Retrieval feels worse (off-topic chunks, missing obvious answers).
- Adding or flipping a retrieval stage in `.env`: `RETRIEVAL_MODE`,
  `RETRIEVAL_REWRITE`, `RETRIEVAL_MULTI_QUERY`, `RETRIEVAL_HYDE`,
  `RETRIEVAL_RERANK`, `RETRIEVAL_K`, `RETRIEVAL_FETCH_K`.
- Changing chunking (`chunk_size` / `chunk_overlap` in
  [src/agents/vector_store.py](../../../src/agents/vector_store.py)).
- Refreshing the router policy after model pulls.
- Investigating an eval regression in `evals/results/`.

## Procedure

1. **Establish baseline.** Run a smoke eval and note the numbers:
   ```powershell
   .\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m evals.run_evals --limit 6
   ```
   Read the freshest `evals/results/results_*.json` and `summary.md`.
2. **Form one hypothesis.** Name the symptom (e.g. *"complex queries miss
   the prepayment table"*) and the knob you'll change (e.g. *"enable
   `RETRIEVAL_RERANK`"*). One knob per run — see
   [references/retrieval-knobs.md](./references/retrieval-knobs.md) for the
   full menu, defaults, and what each one costs.
3. **Change it in `.env`.** Toggles are read on every retrieval call, so no
   restart is needed for retrieval flags. Restart Streamlit **only** for
   router policy or model changes.
4. **Re-run evals.**
   ```powershell
   .\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m evals.run_evals --limit 6   # smoke
   .\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m evals.run_evals             # full
   ```
   See [references/evals-workflow.md](./references/evals-workflow.md) for
   how to read the scored output and what counts as a real improvement.
5. **Decide.** Compare judge / coverage / latency against baseline. Keep
   the change only if it wins on judge or coverage **without** regressing
   the other beyond `quality_tolerance` (see `policy.json`).
6. **Refresh the router policy** if model or chunking changed:
   `evals/results/policy.json` is rewritten by the harness — restart
   Streamlit to pick it up (the router caches at process start).
7. **Commit the result file** so the run is reproducible.

## Quick checks before running evals

| Symptom | First check |
|---|---|
| Empty / no chunks returned | `docker exec pgvector psql -U postgres -d financial_rag -c "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM document_chunks;"` |
| All-zero similarity scores | `EMBEDDING_MODEL` / `EMBEDDING_DIM` mismatch with column type — see `ensure_schema()` in [src/storage/db.py](../../../src/storage/db.py) |
| Router won't switch models | `policy.json` cached at startup → restart Streamlit |
| HyDE / multi-query no effect | Flag spelled wrong in `.env` — must be `true`/`yes`/`on` |
| Latency exploded after rerank | Rerank scores N chunks with one large prompt; lower `RETRIEVAL_FETCH_K` |

## References

- [references/evals-workflow.md](./references/evals-workflow.md) — running
  the harness, reading results, what numbers actually mean.
- [references/retrieval-knobs.md](./references/retrieval-knobs.md) — every
  retrieval stage, default, cost, and when it helps.

## Anti-patterns

- **Tuning two knobs at once.** You can't attribute the delta. One change
  per eval run.
- **Eyeballing the UI.** Single queries are noisy. Trust the harness.
- **Editing `policy.json` by hand.** It's regenerated by `run_evals`. Hand
  edits get overwritten.
- **Re-running evals without restoring `.env`.** Forgotten flags from a
  previous experiment will silently bias every later run.
